title: hypericum tetrapterum var. boeticum
intro: Hypericum tetrapterum var. boeticum, also known as spotted St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant in the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and four-winged seeds. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hypericum tetrapterum var. boeticum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant protection. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and minor wounds.
